title: Servo Temperature Fan
subtitle: Smart Cooling with Arduino!
overview: This session introduces students to building a smart temperature-controlled fan using Arduino, a servo motor, and a temperature sensor. Students learn automation, sensors, and smart cooling systems through hands-on activities.

images._note: Add actual project photos, wiring diagrams, student activities, and screenshots.

activity.title: Build a Servo Temperature Fan
steps: Connect servo motor signal pin to Arduino PWM pin
Attach mini fan to servo motor
Open Serial Monitor to check temperature
Increase temperature near sensor using hand heat
Challenge: Change fan angle for different temperatures

quiz.q: What is the main purpose of the temperature sensor?
quiz.explanation: The temperature sensor detects heat and sends data to Arduino.

quiz.q: Which component acts as the brain of the project?
quiz.explanation: Arduino UNO controls all components using programming.

quiz.q: What does a servo motor do?
quiz.explanation: Servo motors move accurately to selected angles.
The servo motor rotates the fan when temperature becomes high.

quiz.q: Which coding platform is commonly used with Arduino?
quiz.explanation: Arduino IDE is used to write and upload Arduino code.
Automation means systems work automatically with sensors and programming.

quiz.q: Which component provides movement in this project?

quiz.explanation: Breadboards help build circuits without soldering.

quiz.q: Which sensor is commonly used in temperature projects?
quiz.explanation: LM35 is a popular temperature sensor.

quiz.q: What type of project is a smart fan?
quiz.explanation: Smart fans work automatically using sensors and coding.
Arduino UNO can be powered using USB or adapters.

quiz.q: Which part connects electronic components together?
quiz.explanation: Jumper wires connect different parts of the circuit.

quiz.q: What happens when the sensor detects cool temperature?
quiz.explanation: The fan slows down or stops when temperature becomes low.

quiz.q: Which skill improves through this project?
quiz.explanation: Students improve coding, electronics, and creative problem-solving skills.

quiz.q: What is the real-life use of temperature-controlled systems?
quiz.explanation: Temperature-controlled systems are used in many smart devices and industries.
